blocks.title: Apache Kafka Basics
blocks.description: Brokers, topics, partitions, producers, consumers, consumer groups, offsets, retention, and stream processing overview.

blocks.title: How this page is rendered
items: Declarative blocks are loaded from this JSON content file.
PageBlockRenderer maps each block type to a shared UI component.
The page structure outline is loaded from pageStructures/references/KafkaBasics.md.

blocks.title: Broker & cluster
blocks.body: Kafka runs as a distributed cluster of brokers. Each broker stores partition replicas and serves produce/fetch requests. ZooKeeper or KRaft coordinates cluster metadata depending on deployment mode.

blocks.title: Topic & partition
blocks.body: A topic is a logical stream split into ordered partitions. Producers append records to partitions; consumers read in offset order within each partition. More partitions increase parallelism at the cost of ordering scope.

blocks.title: Consumer group
blocks.body: Consumers sharing the same group.id coordinate partition assignment. Each partition is consumed by at most one consumer in the group at a time. Rebalances occur when members join or leave.

items: Producer serializes a record (key, value, headers) and picks a partition (key hash or explicit).
Broker appends the batch to the partition log and acknowledges based on acks config.
Consumer polls, receives batches, processes, then commits offsets (auto or manual).
Retention policies or compaction govern how long data remains addressable by offset.

blocks.title: Delivery semantics (at a glance)
blocks.body: At-most-once skips retry on failure; at-least-once retries but may duplicate; exactly-once needs idempotent producers, transactions, and careful consumer offset management.
